notes: The engineering layer respects the three commentarial readings without conflation. Shankara's gloss ('with equanimity, i.e. without likes and dislikes') is preserved as 'identical dispositional posture toward all three categories' — preserving the without-rāga-and-dveṣa structure without collapsing the pairs. Ramanuja's reading (knowledge of the self as the basis of unaffectedness; pāpam as transmigratory bondage) is preserved through the explicit naming of 'bondage' as the engineering form of saṁsāra (burnout, blame-displacement, defensive code). Sridhara's structural insight (victory/defeat as the cause of pleasure/pain and gain/loss) is preserved by mapping all three pairs to distinct engineering registers while holding the same dispositional move across all three. The doctrinal stake — pairs preserved, stance the same, NOT pairs collapsed — is the engineering layer's load-bearing claim. The 2.48 ('yoga-stha kuru karmāṇi') foreshadow is honored without the technical term being borrowed: 'arrive identical' is the dispositional content of yoga-sthaḥ stated in the engineering register. The 2.47 mature statement is named explicitly in the iteration log as the doctrine 2.38 seeds. PASS.
